page: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 192 vCPUs powered by AMD processors, mapped to 192 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance relies on a shared CPU allocation model, which introduces potential resource contention compared to dedicated CPU options. Local storage is limited to 1 GB of network-based storage, necessitating the attachment of external volumes for data-intensive operations. The instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and does not feature any GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model provides a high density of virtual cores and memory at a lower hardware class tier, though performance consistency may vary under sustained peak loads. This configuration is designed for highly parallelized workloads that can tolerate shared CPU cycles, such as distributed caching, large-scale web serving, staging environments, and non-critical batch processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is an x86_64 server instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 192 AMD vCPUs and 768.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model with one thread per core, it offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Tradeoffs include a minimal 1 GB of network-based local storage and the absence of GPU hardware. This instance is structured for highly parallelized, non-critical workloads such as large-scale web serving, distributed caching, and batch processing where shared resource performance is acceptable.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is a VX1 Cloud Compute instance featuring 192 AMD vCPUs and 768 GB RAM. It operates on a shared CPU allocation model with 1 GB of network storage for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g is a shared-core instance with 192 AMD vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, built for parallelized workloads.
tagline: A high-density shared CPU instance offering 192 AMD cores and 768 GB of memory for parallelized workloads.

768.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
